methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Solid Power is a public-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised $232M to date. The valuation landscape for solid-state battery and advanced energy storage companies remains sparse at the public market level, with only one relevant public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) identified. The private peer universe is somewhat broader at eight companies, including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion, though these span heterogeneous sub-segments within energy storage and climate solutions.

Standard peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable in this case. The Climate & Energy sector contains 21 same-stage peers broadly, but filtering for methodologically sound comparables—requiring three or more companies at identical or adjacent development stages with similar valuation tiers—yielded insufficient datapoints. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given Solid Power's specialized technology and capital intensity requirements.

The sparse comps environment and heterogeneous peer set create material uncertainty around any valuation assertion. Public trading multiples cannot be reliably extracted from a single comparable. Without sufficient private rounds or transaction data in the immediate peer set, revenue multiples or cost-per-watt benchmarks cannot be triangulated with confidence. Key dependencies include: the trajectory of solid-state battery commercialization timelines, competitive positioning versus QuantumScape and other semi-public entrants, and capital efficiency relative to peers. Further analysis would require either transaction-level data from recent private fundraises or granular production/cost metrics.
